title: Max Heap Inorder Insertion Bug Fix

statement.markdown:
Given a max heap of size `n`, implement an `insert` method that inserts a new element while maintaining the heap property. However, the current implementation has a bug. Fix the issue to ensure the max heap property is preserved.

Example Input/Output:

Insert 10 into the max heap: [4, 10, 3, 8, 9, 1]
Insert 7 into the max heap: [4, 10, 3, 8, 9, 1, 7]
statement.inputFormat:
List of integers representing the max heap

Example: [4, 10, 3, 8, 9, 1]
statement.outputFormat:
List of integers representing the updated max heap

Example: [4, 10, 9, 8, 9, 1, 7]

hints.text: Review the heapify-up logic to identify the issue.

editorial.title: Max Heap Inorder Insertion Bug Fix Editorial
editorial.intuition: To maintain the max heap property after insertion, heapify-up is required.
editorial.optimalSolution: Maintain a max heap by comparing each new element with its parent and swapping them if necessary.

functionSignature.cpp: void insert(int* arr, int n, int val);
functionSignature.java: public void insert(int[] arr, int n, int val);
functionSignature.python: def insert(self, arr: list[int], n: int, val: int) -> list[int]:

solutions.python:
from typing import List

class Solution:
    def insert(self, arr: List[int], n: int, val: int) -> List[int]:
        arr.append(val)
        i = n
        while i > 0 and arr[i] > arr[(i-1)//2]:
            arr[i], arr[(i-1)//2] = arr[(i-1)//2], arr[i]
            i = (i-1)//2
        return arr

if __name__ == '__main__':
    nums = [4, 10, 3, 8, 9, 1]
    n = len(nums)
    val1 = 7
    val2 = 10
    s = Solution()
    print(s.insert(nums, n, val1))
    print(s.insert(nums, n, val2))
